questions.question:
What is the output of the following program?

 #include < stdio.h >
int tmp=20;
main()
{
  printf("%d", tmp);
  func();
  printf("%d", tmp);

}
func()
{
 static int tmp=10;
 printf("%d", tmp);
}


(ISRO CSE 2017)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What does the following program do when the input is unsigned 16 bit integer?

 #include < stdio.h >
main(){
  unsigned int num;
  int i;
  scanf("%u", &num);
  for(i=0;i<16;i++){
    printf("%d", (num < < i&1 < < 15)?1:0);
  }
}


(ISRO CSE 2017)
options:
A.

It prints all even bits from num

B.

It prints all odd bits from num
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What will be the output of the following C code?

 #include < stdio.h >
main()
{
    int i;
    for(i=0;i<5;i++)
    {
        int i=10;
        printf("%d" , i);
        i++;
    }
    return 0;
}



(ISRO CSE 2017)

questions.question:
What does the following C-statement declare?

 int (*f) (int * );


(ISRO CSE 2017)
options:
A.

A function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns an integer

B.

A function that takes an integer as argument and returns an integer pointer

C.

A pointer to a function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns an integer

D.

A function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns a function pointer
questions.correct_answer:
C.

A pointer to a function that takes an integer pointer as argument and returns an integer
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
Consider the following function

 void swap(int a, int b)  
{       
    int temp;
    temp = a;
    a = b;
    b = temp;  
}
In order to exchange the values of two variables x and y.

(ISRO CSE 2017)
options:
C.

swap(x,y) cannot be used as it does not return any value

D.

swap(x,y) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value
questions.correct_answer:
D.

swap(x,y) cannot be used as the parameters are passed by value
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow

questions.question:
What is the output of the C++ program?

 #include < iostream >
using namespace std;

void square(int *x){
    *x = (*x)++ * (*x);
}

void square(int *x, int *y){
    *x = (*x) * --(*y);
}

int main()
{
  int number = 30;
  square(&number, &number);
  cout < < number;
  return 0;
}


(ISRO CSE 2017)
questions.explanation: Click here for detail solution by gateoverflow
